reviews.review_text:
Please, SEGA.
Just bring back this one out of delisting limbo.

The original Yakuza 0 is widely considered the superior way to experience the game's story, despite the release of the Director's Cut in late 2025, the original story is considered more cohesive. 

Most critics of the Director's Cut point out that its new cutscenes (roughly 26 minutes of added content) introduce questionable retcons and "pointless" additions that can actually detract from the emotional impact of certain scenes.

For many players, the "phone booth" saving system in the original is a core part of its 1980s retro charm. The Director's Cut allows saving anywhere, which some argue makes the experience slightly less immersive.

Please, SEGA.
No more delisting games that are far better than their replacements.

 I accept new versions, but it will be nice if just let the originals and their new versions existing together. 

More profits for you, because veteran and noob gamers, old or young, can enjoy it to each their own.
despite Kiryu and Majima both being men, Kiryu is a type of Kirby and Majima is a type of woman.

It starts off slow and there's a lot of yapping in cutscenes but if you look past that, this is a brilliant game and a good introduction to the series if you're new. Shame on Sega for delisting this in favor of a modern, washed version.

If I'm being honest, it took me a lot of time to reflect and think about how genuinely good this game is, not only as a starting point to anyone who want to get into the Yakuza franchise but also as a standalone game. This game is by far the BEST Yakuza game to use as a benchmark for the others because of how it establishes the Yakuza game format so easily to the player. The issue is that, the first Yakuza game experience you have is always gonna be a cherished one and after playing this one as my first, that is exactly what happened. Now if at anytime I wanna startup a new Yakuza game I start comparing it to the experience I had with this one. Nonetheless, I still believe this game can hold up on its own when it comes to the story (the thing that mainly differentiates these games).
Let me drop a huge load of TRUTH NUKE uranium juice and tell you that MAJIMA (fav character btw) is NEVER gonna be as cool as he was IN THIS GAME. His storyline was so good that it basically inspired a screenplay I wrote for one of my college assignments in a screenwriting course. I'm not saying it carries the game but its generally better than the Kiryu stuff but he's still not bad. Other than that the leveling using money system is kinda bad. Of course this being basically the starting point for most people when it comes to Yakuza, it sets the expectation for that Yakuza fun stuff like funny stupid side quests and minigames that are more interesting than the combat itself to appear in future Yakuza games. You've got side quests like the kid who wanted that video game, you buy another kid a porno mag from a vending machine and the weird Japanese cult that's actually based on real life events, just all time classics you know? 
So yea TLDR: good game if you want to dip your toes into yakuza before finally taking the dive and good game on its own feet (-10000 points for the cat fighting minigame).

Yakuza 0 is probably one of my favorite games. You have these small areas where you can do so much and have a grand time out. It might also be the one with my favorite mini-games like disco-dancing, karaoke. This was the game that introduced me to the Yakuza-series and it's so high on my list that the latter still haven't topped it. I especially love the story, how it all revolves around this small piece but goes into an epic scale of betrayal and conflict. The story is ludicrous and at times too convenient regarding the ties the characters have. But the melo-drama and sincerity is what makes it honest to god work!
Honestly, just a very fun game to play and intro to the Yakuza Series.
Runs well and is significantly better on storage space than the directors cut. Got me into the franchise. Radical
Delisted for no reason, and the replacement is nonsense. The reviews say it all.

Trying the yakuza again after +- 15 years. Playing my first yakuza games when I was in elementary school and I didn't even pass the first chapter since I don't understand the mission. Comeback when I'm an adult to complete the yakuza series. Hope I could do it.

Yakuza 0 is a great game and a good start to someone who wants to follow the story from beginning.

Amazing games with great story and fun combat mechanics and interesting characters.
8.6/10
a lot of content and the gameplay is really good.
Love the series, if you love a game with peak story and totally not random side missions, this game is the answer.
Spent 130 hours losing my mind in mahjong for 100%. Peak game, great for the mentally unstable.

Great game. So many good things to say about this. I originally planned to just beat this for the story, but I had so much fun I ended up 100%ing it. 

-Amazing story. So refreshing to enjoy a story that keeps you engaged from the very beginning and holds your attention. The substories are great too and add some comedic effect. 
-Gameplay is solid and diverse between two characters. 
-Fun minigames. I initially only did the minigames for the completionist aspect, but they're so much fun to play. Not to mention climax mode is good for challenges. 

9.7/10
Absolute CINEMA. Mahjong, claw machines, fighting, and karaoke. What else could a game need except MORE mini games.
Probably the best entry in the series. Playing as Majima has got to be the absolute highlight of the game.
Aside from the excellent story and gameplay, this game works flawlessly on the Steam Deck.
Amazing game, despite being over 10 years old it follows an amazing story featuring great and memorable characters.
Prob the second best yakuza game for me, i love the minigame, the story, substory, etc..

It’s my first time playing a Yakuza game so there’s not much to compare it to other than similar games I’ve played but that could only reach me that far.

For a normal mode gameplay, the combat is quite easy, especially if you’re already adept with a preferred battle style and know the combos that deliver much damage. If it weren’t enough, weapons helped too. (Thanks, Mr. Shakedown.)

Side quests kept me busier than the main story itself which was an afterthought until later chapters. I mean, you could forget it when you have either a real estate or cabaret business to grow until you max it. Or have a side quest. Or have some training. Or maxing friendships. But either way, side quests are engaging when you know who to approach and how relevant they could be now or in the future. Took me longer there but it’s worth exploring. In any case, it’s the good start for the Yakuza series, literally setting the bar hopefully. I struggled with the dancing quests and some bowling tricks, but that’s just on the sides and yet, I still got frustrated I couldn’t pass it easily so I decided to skip some side quests (leaving it for post-game exploration).

I lowkey expected too much from this game plot-wise when I shouldn’t. It took shorter than I thought, but it took me that long, only to end far from a closure (even if it’s just the beginning of it all). But either way, it’s a good start.

No joke I think this is a perfect game. This was my first Yakuza game and I literally had zero expectations. The story is genuinely god-tier and all of the characters are well written and memorable. Majima's mini game literally had me in a chokehold.

Actually I lied because the combat really isn't my style (makes my hand cramp up) and Kiryu's mini game was time-consuming and a bit boring. Some of the side quests were ridiculous so I skipped them lol. 

It's still an amazing game nonetheless. It punched its way into my top 5 games of all time!

(Platinumed the game on PS5)

Probably the best game in the Yakuza series so far in my opinion. There's a reason it has the reputation it does.

Yakuza 0 is the game I would tell almost anyone to start with if they want to understand why this series has such a strong fanbase.
I have been a fan of the Yakuza / Like a Dragon series for a long time, and what always stands out to me is how far this studio goes to build a world that feels alive. These games are not just crime dramas. They are playable time capsules. You walk through Japanese city streets, visit stores, eat at restaurants, play arcade machines, get pulled into ridiculous side stories, and somehow go from emotional gangster drama to helping some random stranger with the strangest problem of their life.
That is the magic of Yakuza.
Yakuza 0 is where the story begins, and it gives you a deeper understanding of everything that comes after. You get to see younger versions of Kiryu and Majima, before they become the legends fans know later. The story is serious, emotional, dramatic, and full of betrayal, loyalty, ambition, and pain. It pulls you into the Yakuza world while still giving you enough heart to care about the people inside it.
The cities are incredible. Kamurocho and Sotenbori feel packed with life, food, businesses, nightlife, side missions, fights, and little details everywhere. You are not just running from one mission marker to the next. You are living inside a slice of Japan during a very specific time, and if you play through the series, you really start to see how the world and the studio’s design evolved over time.
The amount of content is almost ridiculous. There are main missions, side stories, fighting styles, character upgrades, money-making systems, mini games, arcade games, restaurants, shops, and side businesses that can eat hours by themselves. This is not a small game. It is an action RPG in the full sense, because you are building your character, learning new moves, making money, improving your abilities, and shaping how you fight.
The combat is also a huge part of what makes it fun. Kiryu and Majima both have different fighting styles, and switching between them keeps fights from getting boring. One moment you are smashing people with raw power, the next you are moving fast, using weapons, or turning a street fight into a full comedy-action scene.
What really makes Yakuza 0 special is the balance. It can be serious enough to feel like a high-quality crime drama, then suddenly hilarious enough to make you wonder what kind of fever dream side quest you just walked into. Most games could not pull that off. Yakuza somehow does it with confidence.
If you are new to the series, give this one a shot. Do not be scared by how many games there are. Start here, take your time, explore everything, and let the world pull you in. You might come for the crime story, but you will stay for the characters, the city, the side content, the fighting, the food, the drama, and the absolute madness hiding around every corner.
Highly recommended. Yakuza 0 is not just a great starting point, it is one of the best games in the entire series.

I disrespected kson by starting the series with this game, but still enjoyed it quite a bit. Yakuza 0 is pretty solid, but not perfect.

I enjoyed battles at the beginning, but the skill tree is locked in a way that the most interesting combos are inaccessible, and the game doesn't tell you how to get them. You also need a TON of resourses to actually obtain those skills, and for that you have to grind either in mini-games or business side of deal, none of which are interesting part of Yakuza by themselves. That leaves gameplay pretty boring, especially when most encounters are completely the same, while the game constantly builds up to each of them narratively as if they are some kind of big thing. By the end of it I hated combat, until 2 final bosses showed up. Even without interesting skills, those fights are intense and held me by the throat.

The story is pretty cool. I kind of wished there was less plot twists, because some of them are pretty weird. Like the thing that [spoiler] Goro Majima is asked to kill one person, but in the end does not follow through. With the situation he's in, he should get killed for that himself, but he just doesn't. Then it is revealed that actually Goro [i]never was[/i] supposed to kill, because [i]they knew[/i] he couldn't do it. Then, at the same time other guys try to steal the target to make a contract with them, only later to reveal that they weren't actually set on it, and [i]it would be easier to kill[/i] instead.[/spoiler]. It [i]IS[/i] super weird, but if you let it slide, Yakuza 0 is, like I said, pretty cool and intriguing.

10/10 masterpiece

Starts slow, but story really ramps up during the second half. Excellent story, loads of side content, phenomenal OST. 

The characters are excellently well-written and developed throughout the game, each with their own values and beliefs affecting how they choose to act and navigate within the Japanese underworld. Both protagonists have their own story and struggle which slowly intertwine as the story progresses. Love the little hidden symbolisms and details they've added too - especially the tattoos each Yakuza character has, portraying their ideals and also acting as a foreshadowing device in few instances.

Cinematics are really well done - really did feel like I was on the edge of my seat watching a movie in many instances. And the dynamic intros are just the perfect cherry on top ([spoiler]Everytime I see the Kuze vs Kiryu Sewer Scene with Pledge of Demon playing in the background, I pop a boner of pure testosterone - has to be one of my favourite scenes in gaming. Honourable mentions to the Kiryu vs Shibusawa fight[/spoiler]).

I started Yakuza 0 thinking it was basically just a meme game. Every clip I had seen online was someone singing karaoke, doing weird side quests, or beating people up with random objects, so I thought the whole game was just comedy and chaos.

But the more I played, the more I realized how good the storytelling actually is. Under all the bs moments there’s a genuinely emotional story with characters that you get attached way too easy, especially Majima and Kiryu. Some scenes honestly hit way harder than I expected.

What I love most is how the game mixes serious drama with all the funny moments without either one feeling out of place. It keeps the game entertaining the whole way through while still telling a story you actually care about.

I also think it’s a great way to get into Japanese storytelling and action games. The setting, atmosphere, characters, and overall vibe feel really unique compared to most games I’ve played.

The game can feel a bit heavy or slow at times, but it’s 100% worth it in the end. I went in expecting a funny meme game and left understanding why so many people consider it a masterpiece.

Now time for Yakuza Kiwami.

Please don't downvote this game just because a bunch of d**khead corporate knob goblins at Sega decided it was better to delist a classic game and force feed us a rebranded new one with a higher price. This game is a friggin masterpiece and one of the greatest accomplishments by RGG Studio. Everyone should experience it.

I am in absolute awe with this game. The storyline, the side stories, the deep, funny and unthinkable aspects of this game as well as the dialogues in this game are top-notch! I enjoy playing this game way too much and I highly recommend anyone to have a try. Just be prepared for all the randomness this game throws at you :D
Broke my entire computer upon the simple act of disconnecting headphones. It is shameful this bug is still in the game

The game is amazing. The story is absolutely captivating, well-written and the side content doesn't feel like a waste of time at all. BUT with whatever RGG has been doing recently, I simply can't post a positive review. Creating remakes which are just plain cash grabs with no quality and then delisting the original games from steam is just bound to be met with criticism no matter how good the games are (I'm talking about Kiwami 3 here, but this also applies to Kiwami 1 and 2, what even was the point of making them "Legacy" editions and then selling versions which don't change anything for an inflated price)
I can't believe this karaoke game came with a compelling crime drama story around two protagonists.

I've completed 4 of 18 chapters and I know I'll never finish it. I get it why for some people yakuza series is an absolute favourite of all time but for me it's a waste of time. During my playthrough I tried doing as much side content as possible -- as people on the internet suggest -- and it dragged engaging but not that deep and original setup to a gangster story to roughly 15 hours. That's good, yakuza fans would say, I experienced fun minigames (gotta say, they were pretty fun, but for like 20 minutes, not for mastering all of them, what is encouraged and expected for a platinum trophy, I want to play yakuza 0, not reading 129 pages of mahjong rules lol) and side stories which are a true sauce of these games. I've done about 15 of them I think and there were two that really stuck in my mind. In one Kiryu becomes a movie director for a day; it lasts 17,5 minutes (according to a yt video) and does not contain any gameplay, there're just cutscenes interrupted by a sporadic response choice. And there's no bad answers, different choices just lead to slightly different dialogues. I admit, the dialogues are pretty funny and wholesome but come on, if I wanted to watch a funny sketch I'd just watch an anime or sitcom, that way I'd at least have a voice acting lol. The other side quest is about a little boy who waited all day in a queue and spent his all money for some new video game. After buying it he's mugged by a teenager who stoles his game. You need to catch the thief up and beat him just to find out the game was already stolen by a gangster. So you need to catch the thief up and beat him just to find out the game was already stolen by a yakuza member. So you need to catch the thief up and beat him just to find out the yakuza member is the boy's father who wanted to stole the game his son dreamed about just to make him happy :3. The father and the son reunite and go home to together play a video game that's hopefully more fun than yakuza 0. That mission at least have some gameplay, but this time the story is so cliché, so dull, uninteresting and predictable I couldn't care less about it. I don't know, guys, maybe these 15 missions were the absolute worst ones and all of the other are absolute bangers, maybe the main story becomes the most gripping and original story of all media. Maybe, I guess I'll never know. I wanted to like yakuza and finish all of these games (I've already bought one or two more some time ago...) but honestly after these 15 hours with yakuza 0 I'd need to be paid to even consider wasting that much time
Absolutely thrilling a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ banger. Great story, super fun side stuff, legendary soundtrack. Two story lines combining into one??! Just perfect!
This game is absolutely perfect. The ending had me bawling like a baby.
